body_txt: A commercial service which claims to match strangers with compatible romantic partners through the use of technology. Older Than They Think , with brick-and-mortar shops offering computer and video dating services to clients in the 80s prior to the inception of the personal computer. Other off-line examples include newspaper "lonely hearts" sections and 1-900 premium rate dating phone lines. This trope perpetuates the assumption that normal people find partners organically, and only weirdos, creeps, lunatics, and other folks with insurmountable character derangements would need to resort to "artificial" means of connections. It was particularly prevalent in The '90s when the Internet started making its way into everyday life, and producers were jumping on the New Media Are Evil bandwagon. Unlike modern dating apps where photos are the norm and a faceless profile is a red flag, online image sharing was rare as smartphones were non-existent and digital cameras and scanners were not commonplace. Everyone on the Internet was perceived as a weirdo, creep, or lunatic, and meeting a netizen in real life was considered ludicrously reckless. Naturally, characters who patronized these services suffered the fate of being matched with the least desirable date. Classic examples include :  In modern works, the date is usually a "catfish" who intentionally decieves potential matches with fake photographs and lies. Other scenarios include: There are many other permutations and possibilities available, but no matter whom your suitor might turn out to be, the odds are very high that your first date will be anything but typical. If you're using an international dating or marriage agency , then expect an even bigger minefield, sometimes with an aesop about how we should stick to our own kind. For a similar service but with face-to-face interaction, try Speed Dating . For more general dating disasters, see Bad Date . See also Social Media Is Bad . Internet Safety Aesop correlates with this since it's about being careful about who you meet online.
